title: To A Republican Friend, 1848
content:
혻혻혻혻God knows it, I am with you. If to prize
혻혻혻혻Those virtues, priz占폻 and practis占폻 by too few,
혻혻혻혻But priz占폻, but lov占폻, but eminent in you,
혻혻혻혻Man占퐏 fundamental life: if to despise
혻혻혻혻The barren optimistic sophistries
혻혻혻혻Of comfortable moles, whom what they do
혻혻혻혻Teaches the limit of the just and true
혻혻혻혻And for such doing have no need of eyes:
혻혻혻혻If sadness at the long heart-wasting show
혻혻혻혻Wherein earth占퐏 great ones are disquieted:
혻혻혻혻If thoughts, not idle, while before me flow
혻혻혻혻The armies of the homeless and unfed:
혻혻혻혻If these are yours, if this is what you are,
혻혻혻혻Then am I yours, and what you feel, I share
meaning:
This evocative piece by Matthew Arnold, titled "To A Republican Friend, 1848", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
